commands.author: zw-xysk

target.title: fix(cron): classify empty/incomplete embedded results for isolated cron fallback chain (fixes #97115)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97115.

Isolated cron agent runs that use model fallback chains can fail silently: when a fallback model returns a zero-token or empty result (observed with Google Gemini), the harness treats it as success and stops the fallback chain. The cron job then fails with "Agent couldn't generate a response" without trying remaining fallbacks.

Root cause: `runWithModelFallback` was called from the isolated cron executor without a `classifyResult` callback. The normal agent command path provides this callback via `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback`, which detects empty/incomplete results and signals the fallback chain to continue. The cron path omitted this.

## Changes Made

- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts`: Added `classifyResult` and `mergeExhaustedResult` to the `runWithModelFallback` call, matching the pattern used by the normal agent command path.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.payload-fallbacks.test.ts` — 6/6 passed
- **Regression:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.meta-error-status.test.ts` — 5/5 passed

## Real behavior proof

The `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback` function already handles:
- Zero-token/empty responses → classifies as fallback-worthy
- Hook blocks → preserves them as terminal
- Aborted runs → skipped
- Incomplete turns → retried if fallbackSafe
- Results with committed outbound delivery → preserved

Before this fix, the cron path bypassed all of these checks. After this fix, the same classifier used by the normal agent command path is also used by isolated cron runs.

**What was not tested:** Live Gemini cron run (the fix reuses existing well-tested classifier logic).

- [x] AI-assisted
